ops.title: Guides propagation is pull-only: sweep enablers removed, daily-cron fleet audit green
ops.body:
## Decision (user correction, 2026-07-14)

The 45-agent manual render sweep that propagated the Models-v5/effort-xhigh pack change was wasted effort: cc-guides pack changes ride each consumer repo's daily `guides.yml` cron and land fleet-wide within 24 hours. Anything that encourages or enables a manual whole-fleet sweep was removed the same day:

- `guides-lint.yml` header runbook (the `gh repo list … | gh api repos/yasyf/$r/dispatches -f event_type=cc-guides-render` loop) deleted; the comment now states the pull-only rule (commit `c4f04c39`).
- The orphaned `FLEET_DISPATCH_TOKEN` Actions secret on yasyf/cc-skills deleted (leftover from a prior fleet.json dispatch experiment already ripped out of cc-guides). The underlying fine-grained PAT still needs revoking by the user (GitHub settings / 1Password).
- `plugin/guides/README.md` gained a Propagation section: pull-only, single-repo early render via `gh workflow run guides.yml -R yasyf/<repo>` (one repo, never a loop), plus the 60-day cron-auto-disable caveat.

What stays: the `repository_dispatch`/`workflow_dispatch` triggers in each consumer stub (and `test_scaffold.py`'s assertion) — removing them across ~38 hand-maintained stubs would itself be a fleet sweep, and they legitimately serve single-repo re-renders.

## Fleet cron audit (same day)

43/43 consumer repos (every non-archived repo with `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock` on origin): stub byte-identical to the repo-bootstrap canonical template, workflow state `active`, latest scheduled run success (cc-sudo has no scheduled run yet — newly enrolled, active, covered by the next cron). Zero repairs needed. Standing failure mode to re-check occasionally: GitHub disables schedules after 60 days of repo inactivity, and scheduled runs don't count as activity.

Related: the v5 rollout note documents the sweep this supersedes as practice — see note e3ce144a; memory `never-sweep-guides-fleet`.
